description: This figure presents a heatmap of the Pearson correlation coefficient matrix for 10 descriptors used for feature selection in machine learning studies of low-density FeMnAlC steels. The 10 evaluated descriptors include elemental contents (Mn, Al, C, Fe), valence electron concentration (VEC), melting temperature (Tm), entropy of mixing (ΔSmix), enthalpy of mixing (ΔHmix), electronegativity difference (Δχ), and atomic size difference (δ). The color scale ranges from -1 (red, strong negative correlation) to 1 (blue, strong positive correlation), with larger circle size and deeper color indicating higher correlation magnitude. Key measured correlations include a 0.98 positive correlation between Δχ and δ, 0.97 correlation between C and Δχ, and 0.98 correlation between C and δ. Features with correlation coefficient greater than 0.95 (Δχ and δ) are eliminated to remove redundant information, improving the robustness of subsequent machine learning models built to predict composition-microstructure correlations including κ-carbide presence and phase fraction in FeMnAlC steels.
